sn7.12:1.2: Then early in the morning, the Blessed One, having adjusted his under robe and carrying his bowl & outer robe, went to the home of the brahman Udaya.
sn7.12:1.3: The brahman Udaya filled the Blessed One’s bowl with rice.
sn7.12:1.4: Then a second time, [on the next day,] the Blessed One, having adjusted his under robe and carrying his bowl & outer robe, went to the home of the brahman Udaya. And a second time, the brahman Udaya filled the Blessed One’s bowl with rice.
sn7.12:1.5: Then a third time, [on the following day,] the Blessed One, having adjusted his under robe and carrying his bowl & outer robe, went to the home of the brahman Udaya. And a third time, the brahman Udaya, having filled the Blessed One’s bowl with rice, said to him,
sn7.12:1.6: “This pesky Gotama contemplative keeps coming again & again.”
sn7.12:2.1: The Buddha: “Again & again they sow the seed.
sn7.12:2.3: Again & again farmers plow the fields.
sn7.12:2.4: Again & again grain comes to the kingdom.
sn7.12:3.2: Again & again lords of giving give.
sn7.12:3.3: Again & again having given, the lords of giving
sn7.12:3.4: Again & again go to a heavenly place.
sn7.12:4.1: Again & again dairy farmers draw milk.
sn7.12:4.2: Again & again the calf goes to its mother.
sn7.12:4.4: Again & again the dullard goes to the womb.
sn7.12:5.1: Again & again you take birth & die.
sn7.12:5.2: Again & again they carry you to the charnel ground.
sn7.12:5.3: But on gaining the path to no again-becoming,
sn7.12:5.4: you, deep in discernment, don’t take birth again & again.”
sn7.12:6.1: When this was said, the brahman Udaya said to the Blessed One,
sn7.12:6.3: Just as if he were to place upright what was overturned, to reveal what was hidden, to show the way to one who was lost, or to carry a lamp into the dark so that those with eyes could see forms, in the same way has Master Gotama—through many lines of reasoning—made the Dhamma clear. I go to Master Gotama for refuge, to the Dhamma, & to the Saṅgha of monks. May Master Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge from this day forward, for life.”
